summary: Solid New Year's show with standout Morning Dew, Samson jam & Ship of Fools
blurb: The first set delivers a tight run of Hey Pocky Way, Big Boss Man and Let It Grow, while the second set shines with Samson & Delilah, Ship Of Fools and He's Gone. Jerry’s guitar is fire‑brand throughout, though his vocals waver early. Overall pacing stays strong despite occasional sound glitches.
review: December 30, 1987 at the Oakland Coliseum found the Dead rolling into a fresh New Year’s run with plenty of high points. The opening set was a well‑chosen mix – Hey Pocky Way and Big Boss Man kicked things off with groove, and later gems like Cassidy, West L.A. Fadeaway and Let It Grow kept the momentum humming. Jerry Garcia’s lead work was on fire during Ship Of Fools, Morning Dew and the All Along The Watchtower climax, even if his vocal phrasing slipped a few times early in the night. Brent Mydland’s Hammond organ added sparkle to Hey Pocky Way and the sprawling Samson & Delilah jam, while Phil Lesh laid down a solid bass foundation that shone on Cassidy. The rhythm section was energetic; Billy’s drumming (with Mickey’s steady pulse) drove the second‑set jams, especially the back‑to‑back stretch of Ship Of Fools into He's Gone. Though the soundboard recordings suffer from occasional muffled vocals and pop glitches, the musical performance remains consistently strong, making this a fan‑favorite show for anyone chasing those late‑80s highs.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the playing (Morning Dew, Samson jam, Ship Of Fools, He's Gone) and highlight strong guitar, organ and rhythm section work. Though a few reviews note sound glitches and occasional vocal slip‑ups, the overall tone is clearly positive across three 'positive' and two 'mixed' sentiments, justifying a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Most complete soundboard capture of the full set, includes all highlighted songs and balances decent audio fidelity with the show's strong performance.

key_highlights: Strong first‑set flow with Hey Pocky Way, Big Boss Man and Let It Grow.
Samson & Delilah jam bridges a rough start into a powerful second set.
Back‑to‑back Ship Of Fools → He's Gone creates a soulful highlight.
Jerry’s guitar shines on Morning Dew and the Watchtower finale despite occasional vocal slips.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivered fire‑brand lead guitar throughout, especially on Ship Of Fools, Morning Dew and the Watchtower jam; his vocals were uneven early, with occasional fatigue or lyrical slip‑ups.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid, melodic bass foundation that anchored the set, highlighted on Cassidy and the groove sections of the second set.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboard work was mixed – early lyrical blunders gave way to strong Hammond organ textures on Hey Pocky Way, Samson & Delilah and other jams.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann (with Hart's support) kept the rhythm driving and energetic, delivering solid drumming that propelled the second‑set jams.
